quick_facts.label: Recruiting authority
quick_facts.value: Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) under the Ministry of Railways conduct centralized recruitment for notified Indian Railways paramedical categories.

quick_facts.label: Latest cited recruitment cycle
quick_facts.value: CEN 03/2025 notified seven paramedical categories: Nursing Superintendent, Dialysis Technician, Health & Malaria Inspector Grade II, Pharmacist (Entry Grade), Radiographer X-Ray Technician, ECG Technician, and Laboratory Assistant Grade II.

quick_facts.label: Vacancies in CEN 03/2025
quick_facts.value: The CEN 03/2025 notice listed 434 vacancies across all RRBs. Vacancies are notification-specific and may be increased, decreased, or cancelled by the Railway Administration.

quick_facts.label: Selection method
quick_facts.value: The cited cycle used a single-stage Computer Based Test (CBT), followed by document verification and the prescribed Railway medical examination; empanelment is merit-based subject to completing those requirements.

quick_facts.label: Latest cited cycle status
quick_facts.value: For CEN 03/2025, the CBT was scheduled for March 11–13, 2026, and the RRB Chandigarh CEN page posted CBT result, cut-off, and score-card information on June 5, 2026. Candidates should treat the concerned RRB website and the applicable CEN as authoritative for subsequent stages and new recruitment cycles.

sections.heading: What RRB Paramedical Staff Recruitment is
paragraphs: RRB Paramedical Staff Recruitment is the centralized Railway Recruitment Board route for filling paramedical positions in Indian Railways. It is not a single permanent course examination: the posts, number of vacancies, zones, age bands, medical standards, qualifications, dates, and terms are set separately in each Centralised Employment Notice (CEN). In the cited CEN 03/2025 cycle, the notified posts ranged from Laboratory Assistant Grade II (Level 3) to Nursing Superintendent (Level 7).
bullets: A candidate selects one RRB and gives preferences only for eligible notified posts and railway zones within that chosen RRB.
The RRB’s role is to recommend suitable candidates; the concerned Zonal Railway issues appointment offers, subject to vacancy availability and final eligibility conditions.
A CBT e-call letter or provisional acceptance of an application is not final confirmation of eligibility.

sections.heading: Eligibility: qualification, age, and medical fitness
paragraphs: Eligibility is post-specific. Applicants must possess the stated educational, technical, and professional qualifications from a recognized institution by the notification’s closing date; candidates awaiting final results are not eligible in the cited cycle. They must also meet the nationality, age, disability-suitability, and Railway medical-standard requirements in the applicable CEN.
bullets: Nursing Superintendent: registered nurse and midwife certificate after a three-year General Nursing and Midwifery course recognized by the Indian Nursing Council, or B.Sc. Nursing; CEN 03/2025 specified age 20–40.
Dialysis Technician: B.Sc. plus a diploma in haemodialysis, or two years of satisfactory in-house haemodialysis training/experience; age 20–33 in CEN 03/2025.
Radiographer X-Ray Technician: 10+2 with Physics and Chemistry plus a two-year diploma in Radiography, X-Ray Technician, or Radio-diagnosis Technology; age 19–33 in CEN 03/2025.
ECG Technician: 10+2 or graduation in Science plus a certificate, diploma, or degree in ECG/cardiology technology; the certificate route must be at least one year; age 18–33 in CEN 03/2025.
Laboratory Assistant Grade II: 10+2 Science plus DMLT or an eligible, full-time Medical Laboratory Technology certificate course of at least one year; age 18–33 in CEN 03/2025.

The CEN 03/2025 age cut-off was January 1, 2026. It provided upper-age relaxations, including 5 years for SC/ST candidates and 3 years for OBC-NCL candidates, subject to the notification’s conditions and certificates.
Medical fitness is compulsory. The post’s B1, C1, or C2 standard may include visual-acuity and other requirements; failure to meet the prescribed standard prevents empanelment for that post.

sections.heading: Examination and selection format
paragraphs: The cited CEN 03/2025 format was a single-stage objective multiple-choice CBT of 90 minutes for 100 questions. Eligible PwBD candidates using the scribe facility received 120 minutes. The question standard is generally aligned to the minimum professional or technical qualification for the relevant post.
bullets: Professional ability: 70 questions / 70 marks.
General Awareness: 10 questions / 10 marks.
General Arithmetic, General Intelligence and Reasoning: 10 questions / 10 marks.
General Science: 10 questions / 10 marks.
Negative marking: one-third of the mark assigned to a question is deducted for each wrong answer.
Minimum qualifying marks in the cited notice were 40% for UR/EWS, 30% for OBC and SC, and 25% for ST, with the stated PwBD provision if sufficient candidates were unavailable.
The recruitment sequence is CBT, document verification, and the prescribed medical examination; final empanelment depends on merit, successful verification, and medical fitness.

sections.heading: How to apply when a new CEN opens
paragraphs: Apply only through the link published on an official RRB website for the relevant CEN. In CEN 03/2025, applicants created or reused an RRB account, used a valid personal mobile number and active email address for OTPs, selected one RRB, chose eligible posts/zones in preference order, completed the live-photo and signature requirements, and paid the fee before the notified deadline. Multiple applications to the same or different RRBs could lead to rejection and debarment.
bullets: Read Annexure A for post parameters and Annexure B for RRB- and zone-wise vacancies before submitting preferences.

Recheck entries before submission: the cited notice stated that no correction could be made after submission, while account-creation details and the chosen RRB could not be changed in the modification window.

For dated fee context only: CEN 03/2025 charged ₹500 for most applicants, with ₹400 refundable after appearing in CBT less applicable bank charges; SC, ST, Ex-Servicemen, PwBD, female, transgender, minority, and EBC candidates paid ₹250, refundable after appearing in CBT less applicable bank charges. Future CENs may differ.

Download city/date information and the CBT e-call letter from the official RRB site; in the cited cycle, city information was to be available about 10 days before the CBT and the e-call letter about 4 days before.
The original CEN 03/2025 closing date of September 8, 2025 was extended by corrigendum to September 18, 2025. Those dates are past and do not establish a deadline for a future cycle.

sections.heading: Preparation strategy tied to the official syllabus
paragraphs: Preparation should prioritize professional ability because it carried 70 of 100 marks in CEN 03/2025. Start by matching your study plan to the syllabus for the exact post, then add daily practice for arithmetic, reasoning, general awareness, and secondary-school-level general science. Use timed objective-question practice because wrong answers attracted one-third negative marking in the cited cycle.
bullets: For nursing posts, revise anatomy, physiology, nutrition, microbiology, pharmacology, pathology, medical-surgical nursing, community health nursing, child health nursing, mental health nursing, midwifery, research/statistics, and nursing management.

For radiography, emphasize radiology and radiation physics, X-ray equipment and image processing, positioning, modern imaging, contrast/special procedures, patient care, quality control, and radiation safety.
For laboratory posts, cover medical laboratory technology fundamentals, microbiology, clinical biochemistry, pathology and histopathology, haematology, and blood-bank topics.

For the common portions, practice arithmetic topics such as percentage, ratio, time and work, algebra and mensuration; reasoning topics such as series, coding-decoding, syllogisms and data interpretation; and general science up to the CBSE Class 10 level.

Use the official RRB mock/practice test when made available, and prepare to take the test in the language option selected in the application; CEN 03/2025 offered English, Hindi, and 13 listed regional Indian languages.

sections.heading: What selection can lead to
paragraphs: Successful candidates can be empanelled for the paramedical post, Railway zone, and production unit allotted according to CBT merit, stated preferences, medical fitness, and vacancies at panel preparation. The RRB does not guarantee a particular post or location merely because it was preferred; appointment follows final verification and an offer from the relevant Zonal Railway.
bullets: For CEN 03/2025, initial basic pay was ₹44,900 for Nursing Superintendent (Level 7); ₹35,400 for Dialysis Technician and Health & Malaria Inspector Grade II (Level 6); ₹29,200 for Pharmacist and Radiographer X-Ray Technician (Level 5); ₹25,500 for ECG Technician (Level 4); and ₹21,700 for Laboratory Assistant Grade II (Level 3). These are dated notification figures, not a promise for future CENs or an estimate of take-home pay.
Shortlisting for document verification and empanelment is based on CBT merit, subject to minimum qualifying marks and the notification’s rules.

Original qualification, identity, category, and other supporting documents are checked at document verification. A candidate who cannot produce required originals on the date of verification can forfeit candidature.

Passing the Railway medical test is essential. The notification states that a candidate who fails the prescribed medical standard will not be empanelled for that post and will not receive an alternate Railway appointment on that basis.
